scope: Mistral-7B and Llama-3-8B exclusions from the tab:dark_subspace residual-above-reconstruction analysis. The validity gate (Methods §3.7) admits reconstruction cosine ≥ 0.90 (strict tier) and [0.85, 0.90) (permissive tier); SAEs with recon cosine below 0.85 are reported as diagnostic context only and excluded from the quantitative claim. Values below are run-time outputs of scripts/dark_subspace/sae_dark_subspace.py against the per-model SAE checkpoint listed in tab:model_details (multiplier 4, layer 16, L1 5e-4, mixed-data corpus).

validity_gate_thresholds.below_permissive: diagnostic context only, excluded from quantitative tab:dark_subspace claim

exclusions.interpretation: The SAE is highly sparse (mean active 3.76 of 16384 features) and reconstructs less than half of the membership signal at the residual stream. Excluded from tab:dark_subspace because the reconstruction is not a faithful enough decomposition of the residual stream to interpret the residual-above-reconstruction ordering as a property of the model rather than of the SAE.

exclusions.reason: recon cosine -0.0334 catastrophically below the 0.85 permissive threshold; the SAE does not learn a faithful reconstruction at all
exclusions.interpretation: The SAE collapses to a near-dense decoder (mean active 3814 of 16384 features) and produces reconstructions essentially uncorrelated with the input residual stream. Excluded from tab:dark_subspace and from tab:fsc_values per the manuscript's app:fsc note 'Llama-3-8B is excluded from classifier-feature analyses because the available SAE checkpoint fails the reconstruction-quality criterion.'

note: Both exclusions are gate-driven. The validity gate is set in Methods §3.7 before the cross-architecture table is inspected, so the exclusion decision is independent of the residual-above-reconstruction outcome.
